title: Pecan pie
subtitle: Sweet pastry case with a golden syrup and pecan filling
excerpt: A blind-baked sweet pastry case filled with a mixture of golden syrup, brown sugar, butter, and eggs, packed with pecan halves. The filling sets like a soft toffee: firm enough to slice but yielding in the centre.
sourceNotes: Pecan pie is associated with the American South and is documented in Louisiana and Texas cookbooks from the late 19th century. The earliest printed recipe in a major cookbook appears around 1898. The filling based on eggs, sugar, and a heavy syrup cooked until set is a documented American confection. This recipe uses golden syrup in place of corn syrup, which gives a similar set with a more accessible ingredient for UK bakers.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: The baked pie keeps at room temperature for 1 day, refrigerated for 3 days. Serve at room temperature.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven for the filling bake; the pastry blind bake uses 200°C. Fan at 160°C for the filling.

glossaryTerms.definition: The filling in a pecan pie sets like a soft caramel as the egg proteins and sugars cook and solidify. It is done when the edges are firm and the centre has a slight, uniform wobble when the tin is gently shaken. The filling continues to firm after the oven. If it is completely still in the oven, it will over-set and become rubbery; if it is still liquid in the centre, it needs more time.

content.text: Pecan pie has a filling that behaves like a very soft set caramel, and judging the
takes practice. The pastry case must be fully blind-baked first so it stays crisp under the dense, moist filling. Golden syrup sets slightly firmer than corn syrup; both work.
Make the pastry and blind bake

Rub the cold butter into the flour and icing sugar until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Mix in the egg yolk and 1 to 2 tablespoons of cold water to form a firm dough. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Preheat the oven to 200°C conventional. Roll the pastry to 3 mm thick and use it to line a 23 cm tart tin. Line with baking paper and fill with baking beans. Bake for 15 minutes, then remove the paper and beans and bake for a further 8 minutes until the base is fully dry and pale gold. Reduce the oven to 180°C.

Melt the golden syrup, brown sugar, and butter together in a saucepan over medium heat until the sugar dissolves. Remove from the heat and leave to cool for 10 minutes. Whisk in the eggs, vanilla, and salt. Scatter the pecan halves over the blind-baked case and pour the filling evenly over them; the pecans will float to the surface. Bake at 180°C for 35 to 40 minutes until the edges are set and the centre has a slight, uniform wobble. Leave to cool completely in the tin before slicing.
